it.name: Availability
it.description: Rate the application's availability based on expected performance level

anchors.1: Inconsistent availability and/or performance level
anchors.3: Mostly available when needed; performance level may vary
anchors.5: Always available when needed, and at the expected performance level

it.name: Support Complexity
it.description: Relative to other applications, how much of the total application maintenance resource dollars are required to support this application

anchors.1: Significantly more
anchors.3: Average

it.name: Support Volume
it.description: Average number of support calls received by staff each week

anchors.1: >10 calls per month
anchors.3: 5 calls per month

it.name: Technical Capability
it.description: The technical capability of the application depends on the language used, integration to other applications, compliance, security, etc

anchors.1: Very limited; aging platform
anchors.3: Medium

it.name: Ability to Upgrade
it.description: Ability to upgrade the application

anchors.1: Very low. Will be costly and cause significant challenges
anchors.3: Medium. Doable but not trivial

it.name: SLA Compliance
it.description: Does the application meet maintenance SLA's

anchors.1: No SLA exists
anchors.3: Meets some expectations

it.name: Application Stability
it.description: Number of Incident/Problem/Change Request tickets submitted for each application

anchors.1: >10 tickets per month
anchors.3: 5 tickets per month

it.name: Disaster Recovery Capability
it.description: Rate the department's technical ability to recover from application failure

anchors.1: No disaster recovery plan required.
anchors.2: No disaster recovery plan defined or in place
anchors.3: Disaster recovery plan documented but not tested
anchors.4: Disaster recovery plan not documented but tested
anchors.5: Formal disaster recovery plan in place, documented and tested

it.name: Scalability
it.description: Can the application scale to support increasing business needs. (i.e. multiple lines or markets, additional products or users)

anchors.1: Not at all. Already reaching limitations
anchors.2: Limited scalability opportunity; will reach limitations fairly soon
anchors.3: Somehow scalable. Will reach limitations in the short-term
anchors.4: Scalable and appropriate for long term

it.name: Adaptability / Extensibility
it.description: Rate the application's flexibility to easily accommodate changes in business rules and processes and/or the ease of adding additional processes and services

anchors.1: Does not have the flexibility to support additional processes or services
anchors.2: Has the flexibility to support minimal additional processes or services
anchors.3: Has the flexibility to support additional basic processes or services
anchors.4: Has the flexibility to support the majority of additional processes or services
anchors.5: Has the flexibility to support all envisioned additional processes or services

it.name: Portability
it.description: Rate the application's capability to migrate functionality to an alternative software product or hardware platform

anchors.1: Low.  The application is rigidly structured to the existing platform
anchors.3: Medium.  Components of the application have the flexibility to be migrated to other platforms
anchors.5: High.  The application supports multiple technical platforms and can be readily migrated

it.name: Quality
it.description: Rate the level at which the application runs consistently and produces accurate results with low to zero defects

anchors.1: Low. Results are inconsistent and not deemed reliable
anchors.3: Medium. Results are sometimes inaccurate or include defects
anchors.5: High.  Results are consistently accurate and of high quality

it.name: Performance
it.description: Rate the application's user response time, process turnaround, and batch timeliness against business expectations

anchors.1: Low. Performance is unacceptable and does not meet business requirements
anchors.2: Performance is inconsistent and sometimes meets buisness requirements
anchors.3: Medium. Performance meets business expectations
anchors.4: Performance meets buisness requirements and is consisent

it.name: Maintainability
it.description: Rate how well the application is documented and understood by the support team

anchors.1: Low. Operating documentation does not exist and the level of knowledge within the support team is low
anchors.2: Some kind of documentation and support team knowledge exists
anchors.3: Medium. Some operating documentation exists and general support knowledge is prevalent
anchors.4: Operating documentation exists for the application and but not for all its components.  Support staff have a good understanding of the application
anchors.5: High. Operating documentation exists for the application and all its components.  Support staff have a deep understanding of the application

it.name: Complexity
it.description: Select the category that best describes the application's level of complexity based on integration with other applications

anchors.1: High / External integration
anchors.3: Medium / Internal integration

it.name: Reusability
it.description: Extent to which the application architecture structure allows it to be accessed or used by other applications. (i.e. service enabled)

anchors.1: None.  The application is not structured to support a services architecture
anchors.2: Supports the sharing of minimal functionalities with other applications
anchors.3: Medium.  The applications uses a mixture of services and point to point interfaces to provide information to other applications
anchors.4: Supports the sharing of majority of the functionalities with other applications
anchors.5: High.  The application is fully service oriented

it.name: Adherence to Enterprise Architecture Standards
it.description: Select the category that best describes the application's alignment to current enterprise architecture standards

anchors.1: Application alignment to Corporate technology standards is unknown
anchors.2: The application is not aligned with current Corporate technology standards
anchors.3: The application is aligned with a minimal number of current Corporate technology standards
anchors.4: The application is aligned with the majority of current Corporate technology standards
anchors.5: The application is fully aligned with current Corporate technology standards

it.name: Disaster Recovery Criticality
it.description: IT perceived importance of the application from a business point of view

anchors.1: Not known or non-sensitive
anchors.3: Non-Critical

it.name: Vendor Support - Database
it.description: Rate the risk of discontinued vendor support for this application's current database version

anchors.1: Vendor does not support the Department's version of the application
anchors.3: Version of application is nearing the end of its support life cycle
anchors.5: Application creates little or no risk.  Version of application are fully supported by vendor

it.name: Vendor Support - Platform
it.description: Rate the risk of discontinued vendor  support for this application's current platform. (i.e. operating system, language, core application)

anchors.1: Vendor does not support the Department's version of the application
anchors.3: Version of application is nearing the end of its support life cycle
anchors.5: Application creates little or no risk.  Version of application are fully supported by vendor

it.name: Vendor Support - Integration
it.description: Rate the risk of discontinued vendor support for this application's integration components. (i.e. WebSphere, IIS, TIBCO)

anchors.1: Vendor does not support the Department's version of the application
anchors.3: Version of application is nearing the end of its support life cycle
anchors.5: Application creates little or no risk.  Version of application are fully supported by vendor

it.name: Security Current State
it.description: Ability to meet security policy and directives. (i.e. the extent to which the application is secured from unauthorized access and users are recognized and not permitted to access unauthorized functions)

anchors.1: Does not meet security policy; high exposure for security risk
anchors.2: Hardly meets security policy; major gaps
anchors.3: Partially meets security policy; manageable exposure for security risk
anchors.4: Meets most of security policy; few gaps
anchors.5: Fully meets security policy; little or no security exposure

it.name: Capacity Constraint
it.description: Rate the required lead time to meet capacity changes for the application  (i.e. adding hardware or software components)

anchors.1: Unable to or can hardly meet capacity requirements
anchors.2: High.  Capacity changes require more than 6 months
anchors.3: Medium. Capacity changes require 3 to 6 months
anchors.4: Low. Capacity changes require 1 to 3 months
anchors.5: Capacity is readily available on demand

it.name: Dependence on Specialized/ Limited Technical Knowledge
it.description: Select the potential exposure to the business due to the need for specialized technology skill sets

anchors.1: Very specialized skill set
anchors.3: Unique skill or knowledge

it.name: Business Criticality
it.description: IT perceived importance of the application from a business point of view

anchors.1: Non-critical
anchors.3: Somewhat critical

it.name: Data Sensitivity
it.description: IT perceived importance of the application data from a business point of view

anchors.1: Non-critical
anchors.3: Somewhat critical

it.name: Supports Multiple Businesses
it.description: Provide the number of businesses that the application supports

it.name: Alignment with Strategic IT Plan
it.description: Rate the application's level of alignment with strategic IT plan (i.e. technology platforms, standards, architecture, skills, etc.)

anchors.1: Low alignment with strategic IT plan
anchors.3: Medium alignment with strategic IT plan
anchors.5: High alignment with strategic IT plan

business.name: Business Units Using The Application
business.description: List the number of business segments using this application

business.name: Functional Alignment with Strategic Business Plan
business.description: Rate the application's level of alignment with strategic business plan

anchors.1: Low alignment with strategic business plan
anchors.3: Medium alignment with strategic business plan
anchors.5: High alignment with strategic business plan

business.name: Importance for Business Units' Programs and Objectives
business.description: Rate the importance of the application for achieving the business units' strategy and program priorities

anchors.1: Not critical
anchors.3: Somewhat critical

business.name: Business Value of the Application
business.description: Rate the value of the application to the business

anchors.1: Low
anchors.3: Medium

business.name: Purpose of the Application
business.description: Please select the main purpose or function for the application

anchors.1: Utility / Administrative Support
anchors.3: Key to run the business

business.name: Owner Satisfaction
business.description: Rate the owner satisfaction with this application

anchors.1: Dissatisfied.
anchors.3: Mostly satisfied

business.name: User Interface
business.description: Rate the application's ease of use

anchors.1: Not at all intuitive - New users require formal training
anchors.3: Some what intuitive - New users require minimal training
anchors.5: Very intuitive - New users require little or no training

business.name: User Satisfaction
business.description: Rate the user satisfaction with this application

anchors.1: Dissatisfied.
anchors.3: Mostly satisfied

business.name: Business Capabilities
business.description: Rate the effectiveness of the application's ability to support required business capabilities

anchors.1: Does not support business  capability requirements
anchors.2: Supports a minimal number of business  capability requirements
anchors.3: Supports some, but not all, business  capability requirements
anchors.4: Supports a majority, but not all business  capability requirements

business.name: Ability to Meet Known Future Requirements
business.description: Rate how well the application meets the business's known future (1yr - 3 yr) requirements

anchors.1: Does not meet known future requirements
anchors.2: Meets a minimal set of known  future requirements
anchors.3: Meets some but not all known future requirements
anchors.4: Meets a majority of known future requirements

business.name: Support of Operational Efficiency
business.description: Rate how the current application provides operational efficiency

anchors.1: Does not provide any operational efficiency
anchors.2: Provide limited operational efficiency across few areas
anchors.3: Provide some operational efficiency across some areas
anchors.4: Provide operational efficiency across most areas
anchors.5: Provide strong operational efficiency across all areas
